target.name: Porotech
target.founder: Tongtong Zhu, Yingjun Liu, Rachel Oliver

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $20M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $1.0B - $11.0B.

private.name: SambaNova Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $11B

data.narrative:
Porotech is a Series A-stage semiconductor company that has raised $20M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparison universe of 11 peers spanning chips and semiconductors: 3 public companies (ALAB, NVDA, AMD) and 8 private firms including SambaNova Systems, Cerebras, Etched, and Tenstorrent.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression assumes a 2.0x step-up multiplier from the $20M total raised proxy, implying $40M. Peer-median triangulation uses three same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables: SambaNova Systems at $11.0B (Series A), Etched at $5.0B (Series B), and Positron AI at $1.0B (Series B). The median of this peer set is $5.0B, with a range spanning $1.0B to $11.0B.

The two methods produce a wide spread. Stage-progression implies $40M, while peer-median implies $5.0B—a 125x gap. This reflects a fundamental tension between the company's early-stage funding history and the extraordinarily high valuations now prevalent in frontier chip design. The triangulated range is $34M (low), $2.5B (median), and $5.8B (high). Key uncertainties include the appropriate peer weighting given the extreme dispersion in recent semiconductor financings, and whether stage-progression remains a reliable anchor in this market environment. The high end depends heavily on SambaNova's $11B Series A valuation as a precedent.
